titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/89

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=383 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:21:15.579599+00 | translation moa=384 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:21:55.614293+00

div.p:
W. Rex Scott' . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . clericis . et laicis . salutem . Sciant presentes . et futuri . me concessisse . et hac carta
mea confirmasse . deo . et ecclesie Sancte Marie de Mailros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . dimidiam carucatam terre quam Ric'.
de london' eis dedit in lessedwin . tenendam in liberam . et perpetuam elemosinam . ita bene . et plenarie . quiete . et honorifice 
sicut carta ipsius Ricardi eis testatur . testibus DauiD . fratre meo . Nicholao cancellario . Alano filio dapiferi . Willelmo de haia .
hugone de exartis . Willelmo de Mortemer . Roberto filio Iordani . Apvd Seleschirche .

William, king of the Scots, to all the worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, sends greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, the half ploughgate of land which Richard of London has given to them in Lessudden, to be held in free and everlasting alms, as well and fully, quitely and honourably, as the charter of Richard himself to them bears witness. As witnesses, David my brother, Nicholas the chancellor, Alan son of the Steward, William de la Haye, Hugh de Essarts, William de Mortimer, Robert son of Jordan. At Selkirk.
